name: Reserve Bank of India
summary: Day 78 of Hormuz blockade. **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; May 15-ending print not yet released. Yesterday's brief specification ('anything above $10bn this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational') remains a PENDING TEST. The recovery is a modest reprieve consistent with temporary easing of pressure around the summit, not regime change — Brent +1.6% over the summit period and USD/INR intraday high of ₹95.9825 on May 15 (close to ₹96 trigger) suggest the relief was indeed temporary. **A10 USD/INR — May 15 intraday high ₹95.9825 = $0.018 (~1.75 paise) from breaching the ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of 5-7 session window)** — RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence; pair did not close at the high (rupee under 'strong selling pressure' per Trading Pedia). **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors** — if announced, partial relief possible. **RBI raised primary dealer trading volumes May 15** (annual trading targets for 21 bond market makers) — liquidity-providing measure; India 10Y stayed flat-to-easing despite global yield shock. **The Bank Nifty -0.8% 1D / -11.9% 3M = RBI REGULATORY DRAG, NOT broad market weakness**: cumulative cost of NOP cap + NDF restriction + FCNR(B) swap window MTM losses. Does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. PRIOR (May 15): **C7 USD/INR ₹95.90 — FOURTH STRAIGHT RECORD CLOSE**; RBI controlling speed not direction — selling dollars near ₹95.96 intervention threshold; pace materially slowed from May 11-12 collapse. **₹96 trigger at Day 3 of 5-7 window — not breached**. At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **RBI WEEKLY RESERVES PRINT DUE ~MAY 16 (FRIDAY) = SUSTAINABILITY TEST**. Last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer. **C8 BANK NIFTY -0.2% / -4.1% MoM / -11.4% 3M = RBI REGULATORY DRAG, NOT BROAD MARKET WEAKNESS.** RBI March 27 NOP cap ($100M) on banks' Net Open Position INR + April 1 prohibition on AD banks offering NDF contracts to corporates = **Q4 MTM losses estimated ₹3,000-4,000 cr on $30-40B outstanding bank positions**; banks' FX revenue capacity structurally impaired. **This is the regulatory-stress signal the macro-prudential framework warns about**: Bank Nifty weakness is NOT broad market weakness — it is one sector absorbing the cost of RBI's currency-defense toolkit. The 3M -11.4% is the cumulative cost of central bank's currency-defense operations to date. **Does NOT reverse on crisis resolution.** FCNR(B) swap window opened at fixed 3.5% for 3yr+ deposits; NRI dollar deposit scheme (2013 playbook, $26B then) under active study but no decision yet. **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — partial relief signal if announced.** Brent +1.6% to $107.42 (refusing to fall on summit); Indian energy import bill pressure persists. India 10Y G-Sec 7.12% (yield easing as traders awaited Trump-Xi summit outcome). **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 MAY 14 MORNING — RECORD CONTINUES, PACE SLOWED FROM MAY 11-12 COLLAPSE**: May 13 close ₹95.31 (4th straight session fresh closing record); RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone holding. Pace materially slowed. **₹96 trigger at Day 2 of 5-7 window — not breached.** At current ~5-7bps/day pace, ₹96 hits May 21-23 if Brent doesn't retrace. **RBI WEEKLY RESERVES PRINT DUE ~MAY 16 (FRIDAY)** = critical signal — last week was -$7.79bn; if this week extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. RBI economists note India can deploy ~$150bn before import cover falls to 2013 levels = meaningful buffer. Brent retraced to $105.68 May 14 from May 13 close $108.05 (single-session $108-110 breach not sustained); relief for India's import bill marginal but real. India 10Y G-Sec ~7.00% (month high — investors cautious ahead of inflation reading; Moody's sovereign cut absorbed). **Decision-relevant**: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% remains GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. Bullish reversal still requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93. PRIOR (May 13): **USD/INR FRESH CLOSING RECORD ₹95.63 MAY 12 (vs ₹95.54 intraday in yesterday's brief); intraday ₹95.58; ₹95.50 RBI direct-intervention threshold NOW BROKEN IN CLOSE PRINTS, not just intraday. May 13 morning ₹95.68 +0.3% / +5bps — pace SLOWED from May 12's +1.2%. RBI macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap + NDF restriction) tested and broken.** **Moody's CUT INDIA FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** = sovereign-rating channel; absorbed by bond market without further spike (10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat). Reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16; markets watching for >$10bn weekly draw as intervention-sustainability question. **NEXT TEST ₹96 within 5-7 sessions if Brent doesn't retrace** = trigger now Day 1 of 5-7 window. Decision-relevant: RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% is GONE in current regime — cuts accelerate FII exit. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **RBI'S ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD CLEANLY BROKEN — INR ₹95.54/USD MAY 12 (intraday). The macro-prudential toolkit (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction operational since May 5) has been TESTED AND BROKEN.** Trigger 'USD/INR crosses ₹95/USD' RE-BREACHED after May 6 reversal. **RBI FOREX RESERVES -$7.79bn TO $690.69bn WEEK ENDED MAY 1 — sharp drawdown, evidence of active intervention; reserves down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak $728.49bn** (~10.5mo import cover, still safe but visibly draining). The curbs slowed but did not stop the move. Bond market: India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% — auction-anchored despite crude bid. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: Brent $105.58 → CAD widening → FII -₹8,437 cr single day (highest since April 24) → ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD (exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr) → reserves drain → weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure.** Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address (defer gold/foreign travel/fertilizer cut/edible oil restraint) explicitly targets FX pressure points (gold imports ~$50bn/yr drain) — first major Indian-government public engagement of cycle. RBI now in position where the next test is ₹96 within 5-7 sessions (markets dossier C2). **Decision-relevant signal:** RBI structural room to ease policy from 5.25% is GONE in current regime — cuts would accelerate FII exit. Bullish reversal requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII flow turn positive + INR retracement to ₹93 handle. None imminent. FY27 GDP forecast cut 6.7%→6.2% by global brokerage — energy shock 'historically large with asymmetric macro risks.'

active_triggers.context: NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction held INR through ₹95.36 record May 5; ₹95.50 threshold UNTESTED. If kinetic restart or FII shock accelerates beyond ₹2.5 lakh crore CY26, threshold becomes the next test.

recent_signals.headline: RBI ₹95.50 DIRECT-INTERVENTION THRESHOLD CLEANLY BROKEN — INR ₹95.54 May 12 intraday record; toolkit (NOP cap $100mn/day + INR NDF restriction) TESTED AND BROKEN; RBI FOREX RESERVES -$7.79bn TO $690.69bn WEEK ENDED MAY 1 (down ~$38bn from late-Feb peak $728.49bn = active intervention not stopping); India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99% (new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95%, auction-anchored despite crude bid); FII -₹8,437.56 cr single-day May 11 (highest since April 24); YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 lakh cr (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr); DII absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address explicitly targets FX pressure (defer gold purchases 1 year + foreign travel 1 year + cut fertilizer 50%). FY27 GDP forecast cut 6.7%→6.2% by global brokerage. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + INR ₹93. Next test ₹96 within 5-7 sessions.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs (primary)
recent_signals.verification: INR ₹95.54 + ₹95.50 RBI threshold break = CONFIRMED multi-source; RBI reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 = CONFIRMED (RBI primary); FII flow data = CONFIRMED (NSDL primary); India 10Y auction cut-off 6.95% = CONFIRMED; FY27 GDP downgrade = CONFIRMED (global brokerage call)
